name: Danica Roem
subject: Returning the favor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek,

When I decided to run for office, it was for one simple reason: to help my community. I wanted to fix Route 28 and make commuting -- and life -- just a little bit less of a hassle for the people of Prince William County.

I wasn't sure how my decision to run would be perceived by everyone else, and to be frank, I didn't really care. I didn't get in this to get publicity or attention. I got into it because I was sick of the way things were being run, and I thought I could get some stuff done in Richmond. But even though I didn't ask for it, I got a bit of attention. And not just from the news outlets -- from leaders right here in Virginia who decided that they liked the way I was doing things. **[One of those leaders was Tim Kaine.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=067PUs2%2BCo5UR7B8AikKqaNtzHyvZogQ)**

One day this fall, Tim stopped by our office to campaign with me and talk to volunteers who were getting out the vote. I said to the person standing next to me, "Any time that Tim Kaine comes into your office, that's a good day." And I really meant it -- not just because it's helpful to have a U.S. Senator campaign with you (of course it is), but because I know Tim is the kind of person who has your back, rain or shine. Having him on my side wasn't about having a "big name" visit the office. It was about friendship, and community, and mutual respect.

So now that it's time for him to run again, I want to return the favor and help him the same way he helped me. I want to have his back, because I know he's the right person to represent Virginians in the Senate. Will you join me?

Click here to pitch in $5 -- or whatever you can afford -- before the big year-end deadline to show Tim that you and I have got his back, no matter what:

**[https://timkaine.com/year-end-deadline](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=5ARcofoHrsUKhuXbTetFx%2FYiFE2IFkm4)**

Thanks,

Danica Roem

| | [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=fVl9WN6Fxwii84LCejvaQ%2FYiFE2IFkm4)
---
| Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---

name: Keith Ellison
subject: Will you join our movement, Derek?
body:
When we organize in our communities, there is nothing we cannot accomplish together.
---
**Pitch in $3 or whatever you can before our end-of-year FEC deadline to help Democrats compete and win in all 50 states.
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3006/1100985930/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")**
**
A common saying I've always kept in mind is that "all politics is local."

That's why Tom and I have been promising you all year that there's a new DNC in town that is investing in every community across this country. We're getting back to basics, and investing in the power of true grassroots organizing.

It's taken a lot of work, but the ground game we all contributed to in Virginia and Alabama led us to victory. When we build power at the local level, we are unstoppable.

**The only way to defeat the Republicans is by building a national, people-powered movement prepared to take them on. That's the winning mindset we need to have going into 2018 -- and that's why I am asking you to step up before our important FEC fundraising deadline:**

[QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3007/1100985930/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3004/1100985930/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3005/1100985930/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3002/1100985930/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3003/1100985930/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3000/1100985930/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
Investing locally allows us to talk to voters about what's going on in their communities, and what changes they need to better support themselves and their families.

Investing locally means having more folks on the ground spreading the word about strong Democratic candidates who are determined to fight for our values.

Investing locally includes developing the tools and infrastructure we need to bring more people into the democratic process.

Derek, grassroots organizing in our communities is how we win. It's what we'll need to do next year to take back the House, gain seats in the Senate, and stop Trump's agenda in its tracks.

**Chip in $3 or more today -- before our end-of-year FEC fundraising deadline on Sunday -- to say you're ready to help Democrats win big nationwide in 2018.**

[ DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c3001/1100985930/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/december-deadline-2017?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd")
Thanks,

Keith

Keith Ellison
Deputy Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c300e/1100985930/VEsP/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c300f/1100985930/VEsHBQ/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c300c/1100985930/VEsHBA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523d44/4067b0a/4865f41a6/7e5c300d/1100985930/VEsHBw/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=12+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20171227_ke_nd&source=em_20171227_ke_nd&refcode=em_20171227_ke_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Roy Cooper
subject: One final time
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
When I was sworn in at 12:01 a.m. last January 1st, I said I would work my hardest at improving the lives of all North Carolinians.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12031a9/2fde7c66/4714677a/1569f9e4/530476384/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/171221-cooper-eoy?utm_campaign=em17_171221_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&refcode=em17_171221_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__RC&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=1")

Hello,

When I was sworn in at 12:01 a.m. last January 1st, I said I would work my hardest at improving the lives of all North Carolinians.

As our first year winds down, and we look back at what we have accomplished so far, a couple of things stand out:

We have announced thousands of new good-paying jobs -- the most announced in any one year for the last decade.

We pushed for a new raise for teachers and laid out the vision for making North Carolina a Top 10 Educated State by 2025. We repealed HB2 and signed the most comprehensive non-discrimination order in state history, working to remove that stain from North Carolina's reputation.

We opposed offshore drilling and said "We Are Still In" on the Paris Accords. We enacted an Opioid Action Plan to combat the opioid crisis plaguing our communities and continued working to help those still recovering from Hurricane Matthew.

But this is just the beginning -- I've always said that my election was the first step. Now we work on phase two: breaking the majority.

**Our end-of-year deadline is approaching. We set an ambitious goal of raising 50,000 before our final 2017 fundraising deadline. I'm counting on you to help us get there:** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$5 »**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12031a9/2fde7c66/4714677a/1569f9e7/530476384/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiZTNzZ1lYTnJJSDE5IjoiNSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/171221-cooper-eoy?utm_campaign=em17_171221_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&refcode=em17_171221_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__RC&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&express_lane=true&amount=5&utm_content=2+-++ask++raquo")

2018 is going to be an exciting journey -- and we have big plans.

But we won't be able to execute our plans if we don't have the resources. Part of the reason for our success in the governor's race in 2016 was we were able to significantly out-raise our opponent.

To be successful in the 2018 elections, we are going to need to have that same advantage.

**[Can I count on you to chip in before our end-of-year deadline?](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12031a9/2fde7c66/4714677a/1569f9fb/530476384/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/171221-cooper-eoy?utm_campaign=em17_171221_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&refcode=em17_171221_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__RC&utm_content=6+-+Can+I+count+on+you+to+chip+in+before+our")**

Roy

I'm counting on you to help us get there: If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | "" | | "" | "" | | "" 2018 is going to be an exciting journey -- and we have big plans. But we won't be able to execute our plans if we don't have the resources. Part of the reason for our success in the governor's race in 2016 was we were able to significantly out-raise our opponent. To be successful in the 2018 elections, we are going to need to have that same advantage. "" It's an honor serving as your governor. Thanks for all you continue to do, Roy | "" * Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina Roy Cooper for North Carolina PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 United States

name: LegitAction Team
subject: Our chance in 2018
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

Everywhere LegitAction members got involved in 2017, we made a serious
difference in the fight to restore our democratic legitimacy.

Since our launch in March, Legit members have fought back against
voter suppression, felon disenfranchisement, and hyperpartisan
gerrymandering. And we’ve brought much-needed momentum to pushes for
automatic voter registration, campaign finance reform, and a national
popular vote.

Together, we’ve built something special: a bold and progressive new
resistance dedicated to protecting our democracy in the long fight
ahead, not just reacting to the Trump scandal of the day.

We can bring about landmark victories for legitimacy that affect
elections in 2018 and beyond, but only if we go into next year fully
prepared. And we’re still $12,045 away from reaching our financial
goal to end the year.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/3555?t=1002&akid=488%2E356062%2EQSc9Hn ]Contribute $3 now. Step
up to help reach our goal of $15,000 before the year ends.

This year, Legit members stood up to emerging national threats while
putting our muscle behind advocacy efforts in states throughout the
country.

We confronted the sham Trump-Pence election commission as soon as it
formed. Signatures and calls from thousands of Legit members helped
create the intense scrutiny that has so far kept the commission from
undermining the vote.

But because of the gridlock and corruption tying up Washington, we’re

body:
**NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester Statement on Democrat Silence on Representative Kathy Rogers**
**For Immediate Release:** 12/27/2017
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement on Democrat silence since Representative Kathy Rogers pleaded guilty to assault:
"It has been five days since Democrat Representative Kathy Rogers of Concord has pleaded guilty to assaulting an election recount volunteer. Surprisingly, her unhinged behavior is still being enabled by top Democrats such as House Minority Leader Steve Shurtleff and Democrat Party Chair Ray Buckley. We take the personal safety of our elected officials seriously and it is clear that Kathy Rogers poses a physical threat to her colleagues. New Hampshire’s Democrat leaders should join me in calling for Rep. Rogers’ resignation before it is too late.”
###

subject: Values
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3208/9e00690/402c4651/2403c0f3/1851696699/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em17_171227_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&source=em17_171227_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__AG")
Hello,

Growing up, my mom was a school bus driver and my dad was a construction worker. While we weren't the wealthiest family by any means, they taught me the importance of hard work. My grandmother, who didn't have a formal education herself, taught me the importance of getting my education.

**These values drive me every single day.**

I know we need to create jobs in Florida that pay a living wage for every family, no matter where they are on the income ladder. I know that everyone deserves access to a quality public education. I know that when people work together, anything is possible.

**[We will make Florida into a state that works for everyone, but we've still got a tough journey ahead. So can you pitch in $5 before my end-of-year deadline on Sunday?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3208/9e00690/402c4651/2403c0f2/1851696699/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-eoy-2017?utm_campaign=em17_171227_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171227_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&utm_content=2+-+We+will+make+Florida+into+a+state+that+w")**

I knew it would take a lot of power, hope, and determination to help rebuild Florida after Governor Rick Scott's eight years in office. But thanks to grassroots supporters like you, I'm more confident than ever that we can do it. Together.

**[So chip in $5 now before our critical end-of-year deadline on Sunday and let's end 2017 on a high note.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3208/9e00690/402c4651/2403c0f5/1851696699/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-eoy-2017?utm_campaign=em17_171227_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171227_EOY1_fnd_gq_ful__AG&utm_content=3+-+So+chip+in++ask++now+before+our+critical")**

Thank you,

Andrew

name: Ellen R. Malcolm, EMILY's List
subject: Déjà vu
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c68bc/24c69fd9/1464648b3/1893ed6b/301958602/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact&source=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact")
---

Peter,

I've been having a major case of deja vu this year.

2017 has a lot in common with 1992 -- the so-called "Year of the Woman" when women were so outraged by the treatment of Anita Hill that they helped send four EMILY's List women to the Senate and 20 to the House.

In '92, women were determined to change the direction of the country. And they're doing the same thing today.

Millions of us joined the Women's March. We swept a tide of women into office this year in special elections and on Election Day. And over 25,000 women have reached out to EMILY's List this year to learn about running for office -- compared to just over 900 from all of last cycle!

The tide is changing, and women are the driving force behind it all.

**[Donate $3 to become a 2018 EMILY's List member and make next year one for the history books.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c68bc/24c69fd9/1464648b3/1893eb30/301958602/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171220_email_EOY-membership?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+if+consinConsGroup9050+to+renew+y&utm_campaign=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact&source=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact")**

When Anita Hill came forward 26 years ago about then-Supreme Court nominee Clarence Thomas sexually harassing her, it was the first time we as a country really had a national conversation about sexual harassment.

And it would never have happened if seven congresswomen hadn't marched down to the Senate and demanded that Anita be heard.

A few days later, the images of an all-white, all-male Senate Judiciary panel questioning an African American woman about the validity of her experience sparked a movement. The next year, EMILY's List membership increased 600%, helping to put up some major wins at the ballot box.

It was electrifying.

And the similarities between that moment and this year's Women's March and #MeToo movement are impossible to miss.

Another similarity: We need everyone to give everything they can to use this energy to create lasting change.

**Donate $3 to become a 2018 EMILY's List member so we can take the energy of this year and turn it into big political wins for women:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/2018-Membership-Drive](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c68bc/24c69fd9/1464648b3/1893ed65/301958602/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171220_email_EOY-membership?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorg2018MembershipDr&utm_campaign=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact&source=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact")**

Warmest regards,

Ellen R. Malcolm
Founder and Chair of the Board, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c68bc/24c69fd9/1464648b3/1893ed66/301958602/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171220_email_EOY-membership?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact&source=WQEFRL42N_digital_email_20171227_Like92_inact")

**That 's right, if we're able to add Jacky Rosen of Nevada and Kyrsten Sinema of Arizona to their ranks, Democrats can take back the Senate in November.**

But that's no small task, team. Re-electing all TEN of our female democratic Senators (plus two more) will take all hands on deck.

Pitch in $1 to help the Women's Senate Network make sure all our candidates start 2018 strong.**](http://action.dscc. Democrats will have 49 Senators when Doug Jones is confirmed in January. Just imagine what it would look like if we re-elected all of our Democratic women to the Senate -- and added two more to their ranks.

In the year ahead, we can either elect a record number of women to the Senate or lose nearly half the women in the Senate. What we do now to support these candidates will make all the difference on Election Day 2018.

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: What the Progressive Left have been doing for years…
body:
| | And why we should be doing it too!
---
[ ](http://www3.myfaithvotes.org/year_end_giving?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171227_apl_s1&n=1)
| Dear Peter, Doesn’t it seem like conservative Christians are often one step behind the tactics of the progressive left? This isn’t because we aren’t as passionate or don’t work as hard to defend the values we hold dear. No, I believe it’s because we have failed to utilize technology as well as they do. The Progressive Left is efficiently reaching millions online through advanced digital media and data technologies. Their partnerships with top high-tech companies – like Google, Facebook, Twitter – are enabling them to be louder and stronger! Yet, Christians could easily be the most potent voting block in America. We are 90 million strong... _if_ we simply vote. Christian values in our society are in jeopardy, and we must use new tools to mobilize the Christian vote if we hope to see America stand strong for God. That’s why I’m so excited about My Faith Vote’s Personal Voting Assistant. It’s technology that makes preparing to vote and then voting easy. It is now more possible than ever to see 90 million Christians stand united at the ballot box! **But we need your year-end support to cover our funding gap for this technology.** **[Every gift you give in 2017 will be doubled by generous matching grants up to $50,000!](http://www3.myfaithvotes.org/year_end_giving?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171227_apl_s1&n=2)** When you give today, you empower more Christians to benefit from our state-of-the-art technology, helping them register to vote, request absentee ballots, find their polling locations, and receive reminders to get them to the polls! **Nearly 90% of Christians who used our tools turned out to vote!** This is an extraordinary number far above the national voter turnout rate of 55%. We simply need to be able to offer this resource to more Christians in 2018! **[Give now before year-end, your gift will be doubled by a $50,000 matching grant to help us reach our $500,000 year-end goal!](http://www3.myfaithvotes.org/year_end_giving?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171227_apl_s1&n=3)** Let’s think smarter and utilize technology to empower Christians to stand and vote! **United. We Stand.**

Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171227_apl_s1&n=4) [](http://www3.myfaithvotes.org/year_end_giving?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171227_apl_s1&n=5)
* * *
_My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in local and national elections and be active members of society. By partnering with local churches, pastors, and national faith leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics._
---

2017 has been every bit as challenging as we expected. From fighting to protect access to health care to resisting Betsy DeVos to standing against un-American immigration policies, it's been exhausting for everyone.

But your work and your resistance are not for nothing. Because of you, change is coming. We're seeing it with sweeping Democratic wins in Virginia and a massive upset in Alabama.

subject: We can't imagine 2017 without these Democratic women:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
In 2012, we elected TEN Democratic women to the Senate. Now all ten -- nearly HALF the women serving in the Senate -- are facing re-election in 2018.
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171227-AreYouIn_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team,

In 2012, we elected four new Democratic women to the U.S. Senate -- Tammy Baldwin, Mazie Hirono, Heidi Heitkamp and Elizabeth Warren -- bringing us to a record-breaking 20 women serving in the Senate.

We also re-elected all six of the Democratic women who were up that year: Maria Cantwell, Dianne Feinstein, Kirsten Gillibrand, Amy Klobuchar, Claire McCaskill and Debbie Stabenow.

And now, all ten of those women -- nearly HALF the women serving in the Senate -- are facing re-election in 2018.

[**We can 't afford to lose a single one of our amazing Senate champions this year. Pitch in $1 right now to the Women's Senate Network to re-elect all our Democratic women.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171227-AreYouIn_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

We're so proud of the way our Democratic Senate women have fought for women and working families throughout the last five years.

Like Tammy Baldwin, who has been one of our fiercest progressive champions and a leader in the fight for equal pay.

Or Amy Klobuchar, whose daughter was born with a serious medical condition and who has championed the Children's Health Insurance Program and coverage for people with pre-existing conditions.

Or Elizabeth Warren, who has never missed an opportunity to fight for working families -- and get under Donald Trump's skin.

We can't imagine the fights of 2017 without our Democratic Senate women -- and we know how much we need their continued leadership.

**With TEN Democratic Senate women up for re-election in 2018, we don 't have a moment to waste. Pitch in $1 now to the Women's Senate Network to gear up for the fights ahead:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/re-elect-our-democratic-women**](http://action.dscc. Thank you,
